name: 1MCP Agent
what_it_does: Aggregates multiple MCP servers into a single unified endpoint, simplifying configuration across AI assistant clients like Claude Desktop, Cursor, and VSCode.
use_cases: Consolidating many MCP servers behind a single endpoint for easier client configuration
Managing MCP server lifecycle with health monitoring and hot-reload
Securing MCP access with OAuth 2.1 authentication and scope-based authorization
not_for: Running as an MCP server itself that provides domain-specific tools
Replacing individual MCP servers - it proxies them, not replaces them
Use cases where direct stdio MCP connections are preferred over HTTP
best_when: You have multiple MCP servers and want a single HTTP endpoint with centralized auth and health monitoring.
avoid_when: You only use one or two MCP servers and direct stdio connections work fine for your setup.

auth.notes: Supports OAuth 2.1 with scope-based authorization for production deployments. Can also run without auth for local development.

pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 licensed open source. Free to self-host.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Health check endpoints exist but error handling documentation is minimal in the README. Likely reasonable given TypeScript implementation.
known_agent_gotchas: Acts as a proxy, not a tool provider - agents interact with the underlying servers, not 1MCP itself
Requires HTTP transport - clients that only support stdio need adaptation
OAuth setup adds complexity for local development

security_score_components.security_notes: General agent framework MCP. Security depends on tools exposed. Apply least-privilege to all tool definitions. Validate agent actions before execution in production.
